text:
Once upon a time in a quaint village, there lived a curious cat named Luna. Luna loved listening to old people. They spoke of a wise owl named Oakwing who knew everything about everything, well, except how to cook.

One dusky afternoon, while Luna listened, an old owl fluttered into her perch. “Oakwing?”

”Hello! Do you know how I bake?”

 ”Oh, I’ve heard of you!”

”You bake?”

“Oh! Can’t cook. But I’m a berry connoisseur.”

They gossip’d for hours over acorns. One day, Oakwing said, “There’s a enchanted maple tree. If you pick its biggest berry you’ll have everlasting sweetness.”

”How would I ever afford such things?”

”Let me help you.”

So she did. And all day you’d find her by the berry. That night, eating berries made her owl eyes glow like stars.
